hawkish_phrases: some of the risks from more persistent inflationary pressures may have begun to crystallise
If there were to be evidence of more persistent pressures, then further tightening in monetary policy would be required
annual private sector regular pay growth increased to 7.7% in the three months to May, materially above expectations
Two members preferred to increase Bank Rate by 0.5 percentage points, to 5.5%
successive forecasts had under-predicted inflation persistence
dovish_phrases: Some more recent indicators show signs of weakening
the unemployment rate had increased a little
Twelve-month CPI inflation fell from 8.7% in May to 7.9% in June
One member preferred to leave Bank Rate unchanged at 5%
the risks of overtightening had continued to build
summary: The MPC voted 6-3 to increase Bank Rate by 0.25 percentage points to 5.25%, with two members preferring a 0.5pp hike and one preferring no change, citing crystallizing risks from persistent inflationary pressures particularly in wage growth while signaling further tightening would be required if persistent pressures continued.
